title: wigginsia turbinata subsp. calvescens
intro: Wigginsia turbinata subsp. calvescens, commonly known as the hairy wigginsia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lentibulariaceae. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, where it is believed to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The hairy wigginsia is reported to have therapeutic actions such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, likely due to its bioactive constituents, which include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. However, further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
